title: Flood Warning System in the Everest Region is Broken
summary: A flood warning system in the Everest region of Nepal is broken. The system was built to warn people about dangerous floods from a lake called Imja. The lake is high up in the mountains, over 5,000 meters above sea level. After the lake was drained in 2016, no one fixed the warning system. Now, the towers that have sirens are rusty and some batteries have been stolen. The system also sends alerts to mobile phones, but that part is not working well. Many people live near the lake, and if it floods, they could be in danger. Scientists say that because of global warming, glaciers are melting faster and lakes are getting bigger. This makes floods more likely. Villagers are worried because there have been five floods in the last 50 years. They want the government to fix the system to keep everyone safe.

keywords.explanation: A system that tells people when a flood is coming so they can get to safety.
A large, slow-moving mass of ice formed from snow.
The gradual increase in Earth's temperature, caused by pollution.
A large body of water surrounded by land.
A loud noise-making device used to warn people of danger.

questions.question: What is the flood warning system supposed to do?
Why is the flood warning system not working?
options: It was destroyed by a flood

questions.question: What is causing the lake to get bigger and more dangerous?
How many floods have happened in the Everest region in the last 50 years?
What do villagers want the government to do?

background_read: The Everest region is a mountainous area in Nepal. Many people live there, and many tourists visit. Global warming is causing glaciers to melt, which can create dangerous floods. An early warning system can save lives by giving people time to get to safety.
Article_Structure: WHO: Villagers, scientists, and government officials in Nepal are involved. The villagers are worried, scientists study the glaciers, and officials are responsible for the warning system.
WHAT: A flood warning system that was built to protect people from Imja lake is broken and not maintained.
WHERE: The Imja lake in the Everest region of Nepal, high in the Himalayas.
WHY: The system was left to rust because no maintenance was done after the lake was drained in 2016. Global warming is making the lake more dangerous.

perspectives.description: They are worried and want the government to fix the warning system to protect their homes.
They warn that global warming is causing glaciers to melt and lakes to expand, increasing flood risk.
